headline: DOWNGRADE proposal — band-boundary crossing. Two new in-window negative events: (1) Apr 30 White House blocked Mythos expansion plan (~70 companies/orgs), citing security risks and inadequate compute capacity; NSA retains exclusive access. (2) Mythos breach (Apr 7-21) remediation disclosure remains absent 24 days post-incident — comprehensive structural response not issued. Mitigating factor: Anthropic continues to bear cost of Pentagon autonomous-weapons stance (DC Circuit hearing pending). Composite -1.9 (modest), but composite crosses 60-boundary from Established (61.6) into Functional (59.7). Proposal generated under band-boundary exception to 5pt threshold.
key_evidence: Apr 30 2026: White House blocked Anthropic's plan to expand Mythos access to ~70 companies and organizations. Administration cited security risks and concern about compute capacity adequacy (Bloomberg/WSJ).
Apr 30: NSA retains exclusive Mythos access; broader rollout opposed by administration.

Apr 7-21: Mythos breach via third-party vendor; small group of unauthorized users gained model access. As of Apr 30, comprehensive structural remediation disclosure not issued (transparency dimension gap).
Apr 21: Trump said Pentagon-Anthropic deal 'possible' but no resolution; DC Circuit hearing on merits scheduled May 2026.

Mitigating factor: Anthropic maintains ethical stance against autonomous weapons targeting use even at Pentagon-contract cost — pattern of bearing real cost to maintain commitment (I1 anchor 3 sustained).

evidence.finding: White House opposes Anthropic plan to expand Mythos access to ~70 companies. Administration cites security risks and inadequate compute capacity to serve broader user base without degrading government access.
Detail on government concern: a recent breach during initial rollout heightened anxiety. NSA among current government users; broader expansion blocked.
Unauthorized group accessed Mythos in private online forum during initial rollout; details remain unclear; underlines gap between controlled-access design and practical containment.
AI safety credibility framing of Mythos breach; calls for structured public disclosure that has not yet been issued.
Anthropic blacklisted from Pentagon contracts; DC Circuit hearing on merits scheduled May 2026. Anthropic maintaining stance against autonomous weapons targeting use.

scoring_rationale.AWR: Unchanged at 3.6. Anthropic's harm-detection infrastructure remains active; Mythos breach was detected internally, not via crisis; A1 anchor 4 still applies.
scoring_rationale.EMP: Unchanged at 3.4. No in-window evidence affecting empathy dimensions.
scoring_rationale.ACT: Unchanged at 3.6. Response to breach was technical (containment) but accountability/disclosure scoring belongs to ACC/INT.
scoring_rationale.EQU: Unchanged at 3.1. No in-window equity evidence.
scoring_rationale.BND: Reduced from 3.6 to 3.4 (-0.2). White House block citing 'inadequate compute capacity to serve a larger user base without degrading government access' is a B3 Scope Clarity concern — Anthropic's commercial expansion plan was structurally inconsistent with capacity commitments to existing high-access government users. B5 Consent Orientation also affected: third-party vendor environment breach indicates inadequate vendor-consent boundary. Anchor moves from 4 toward 3.5.
scoring_rationale.ACC: Reduced from 3.6 to 3.3 (-0.3). AB3 Transparency: comprehensive structural remediation disclosure for Mythos breach not issued 24 days post-incident — the company's pattern would suggest annual report disclosure of significant operational failures (anchor 4), but the specific, in-cycle structural disclosure for this material breach has not been made within reasonable disclosure timeframe. Anchor moves toward 3.
scoring_rationale.SYS: Unchanged at 3.8. Anthropic's broader governance systems and frontier-model-forum participation continue; no in-window evidence of systemic learning failures from this breach yet (though future cycles will need to assess AB4 Systemic Learning impact).
scoring_rationale.INT: Reduced from 3 to 2.9 (-0.1). I1 Consistency Under Pressure: Anthropic continues to bear Pentagon-contract cost for autonomous-weapons stance (positive). However, I2 Non-Performance and I4 Values Alignment are weakened by Mythos breach disclosure gap — the company's stated transparency commitments are not consistently applied to its own operational failures. Net modest negative.

band_boundary_rationale: Composite delta is only -1.9 (below standard 5-point change-proposal threshold). However, this proposal is generated under the band-boundary exception: 61.6 → 59.7 crosses the 60-point Established/Functional boundary. Band changes alter the published index entry's classification, so a proposal is required for human review even at sub-5-point delta. Confidence: medium because (a) the Mythos breach remediation may yet be issued in subsequent cycles, neutralizing the ACC/INT pressure; (b) the White House Mythos block is partially a capacity issue rather than purely a compassion-relevant governance failure; (c) per scanner methodology, the DC Circuit Pentagon hearing is pending May 2026 — verdict-pending events should not drive scoring.
negativeSignals: Mythos breach 24 days without structural disclosure; White House block on expansion plan citing security and capacity concerns; third-party vendor environment access exposure suggests inadequate vendor-boundary controls.
positiveSignals: Continued ethical stance against autonomous weapons targeting use even at material Pentagon-contract cost (I1 sustained); breach detected and contained internally rather than via external disclosure; Anthropic remains the highest-published-score AI lab on most dimensions.
sector_context: Per AI Labs sector alert: dual Pentagon AI governance crisis affecting Anthropic, Google DeepMind, xAI/Grok, Palantir AI. Anthropic occupies the unique position in this cluster of refusing autonomous-weapons terms — a positive integrity signal that partially offsets the Mythos disclosure gap. The downgrade is appropriately modest given this posture difference.
open_actions_resolved: Mythos breach: scored on ACC and INT dimensions
White House Mythos block: scored on BND
Pentagon DC Circuit dispute: NOT scored — verdict pending, flagged for future cycle
open_actions_carried: Re-queue Anthropic for next cycle pending DC Circuit hearing outcome (May 2026)
If structural Mythos breach remediation disclosure issued, ACC and INT can be restored
